node.statement: (Correspondence with representation decomposition). The Artin-Wedderburn blocks of π_φ(A)' correspond to the isotypic components of π_φ: π_φ ≅ ⊕_{j=1}^k σ_j^{⊕n_j}, where σ_1, ..., σ_k are pairwise unitarily inequivalent irreducible representations of A, and n_j is the multiplicity of σ_j. The j-th summand M_{n_j}(C) in the commutant acts by permuting the n_j copies of σ_j: for T = (t_{pq})_{p,q=1}^{n_j} ∈ M_{n_j}(C), the action on ⊕_{p=1}^{n_j} H_{σ_j} is (T·(v_1,...,v_{n_j}))_p = Σ_q t_{pq} v_q. This is the content of Schur's lemma applied to each isotypic component: Hom_A(σ_i, σ_j) = 0 for i ≠ j, and Hom_A(σ_j, σ_j) = C·Id.
